title: MiniLM: Deep Self-Attention Distillation for Task-Agnostic Compression of Pre-Trained Transformers
key_claim: MiniLM can compress large pre‑trained Transformer models by deeply mimicking the self‑attention module of the teacher’s last layer—using both attention distributions and a novel value‑relation matrix—and, with a Teacher Assistant, retain over 99% of the original accuracy while using roughly half the parameters and computation.
method_used: Deep self‑attention distillation that matches the teacher’s attention distribution (scaled QK^T) and introduces scaled dot‑product between value vectors as additional knowledge; distillation is applied to the last Transformer layer and optionally staged through an intermediate Teacher Assistant model.
main_result: A 6‑layer, 768‑dim student distilled from BERT‑Base runs 2× faster, keeps >99% accuracy on SQuAD 2.0 and GLUE benchmarks, and achieves competitive multilingual performance, all with about 50% of the teacher’s parameters and FLOPs.
